info.title: Microsoft Defender for Cloud
info.description: API spec for Microsoft.Security (Microsoft Defender for Cloud) resource provider

get.description: Lists all the security automations in the specified subscription. Use the 'nextLink' property in the response to get the next page of security automations for the specified subscription.
List all security contact configurations for the subscription
Get Default Security contact configurations for the subscription

parameters.name: securityContactName
parameters.description: Name of the security contact object

values.name: default
values.description: The single applicable name of the security contact object

put.description: Create security contact configurations for the subscription

parameters.name: securityContactName
parameters.description: Name of the security contact object

values.name: default
values.description: The single applicable name of the security contact object

delete.description: Delete security contact configurations for the subscription

parameters.name: securityContactName
parameters.description: Name of the security contact object

values.name: default
values.description: The single applicable name of the security contact object

get.description: Lists all the security automations in the specified resource group. Use the 'nextLink' property in the response to get the next page of security automations for the specified resource group.
Retrieves information about the model of a security automation.

put.description: Creates or updates a security automation. If a security automation is already created and a subsequent request is issued for the same automation id, then it will be updated.

parameters.name: Automation
parameters.description: The update model of security automation resource

post.description: Validates the security automation model before create or update. Any validation errors are returned to the client.

location.description: The geo-location where the resource lives

etag.description: Entity tag is used for comparing two or more entities from the same requested resource.

AutomationAction.description: The action that should be triggered.

actionType.description: The type of the action that will be triggered by the Automation

AutomationActionEventHub.description: The target Event Hub to which event data will be exported. To learn more about Microsoft Defender for Cloud continuous export capabilities, visit https://aka.ms/ASCExportLearnMore

eventHubResourceId.description: The target Event Hub Azure Resource ID.

sasPolicyName.description: The target Event Hub SAS policy name.

connectionString.description: The target Event Hub connection string (it will not be included in any response).

isTrustedServiceEnabled.description: Indicates whether the trusted service is enabled or not.

AutomationActionLogicApp.description: The logic app action that should be triggered. To learn more about Microsoft Defender for Cloud's Workflow Automation capabilities, visit https://aka.ms/ASCWorkflowAutomationLearnMore

logicAppResourceId.description: The triggered Logic App Azure Resource ID. This can also reside on other subscriptions, given that you have permissions to trigger the Logic App

uri.description: The Logic App trigger URI endpoint (it will not be included in any response).

AutomationActionWorkspace.description: The Log Analytics Workspace to which event data will be exported. Security alerts data will reside in the 'SecurityAlert' table and the assessments data will reside in the 'SecurityRecommendation' table (under the 'Security'/'SecurityCenterFree' solutions). Note that in order to view the data in the workspace, the Security Center Log Analytics free/standard solution needs to be enabled on that workspace. To learn more about Microsoft Defender for Cloud continuous export capabilities, visit https://aka.ms/ASCExportLearnMore

workspaceResourceId.description: The fully qualified Log Analytics Workspace Azure Resource ID.

value.description: The Automation items on this page

nextLink.description: The link to the next page of items

AutomationProperties.description: A set of properties that defines the behavior of the automation configuration. To learn more about the supported security events data models schemas - please visit https://aka.ms/ASCAutomationSchemas.

isEnabled.description: Indicates whether the security automation is enabled.

scopes.description: A collection of scopes on which the security automations logic is applied. Supported scopes are the subscription itself or a resource group under that subscription. The automation will only apply on defined scopes.

sources.description: A collection of the source event types which evaluate the security automation set of rules.

actions.description: A collection of the actions which are triggered if all the configured rules evaluations, within at least one rule set, are true.

AutomationRuleSet.description: A rule set which evaluates all its rules upon an event interception. Only when all the included rules in the rule set will be evaluated as 'true', will the event trigger the defined actions.

scopePath.description: The resources scope path. Can be the subscription on which the automation is defined on or a resource group under that subscription (fully qualified Azure resource IDs).

AutomationSource.description: The source event types which evaluate the security automation set of rules. For example - security alerts and security assessments. To learn more about the supported security events data models schemas - please visit https://aka.ms/ASCAutomationSchemas.

ruleSets.description: A set of rules which evaluate upon event interception. A logical disjunction is applied between defined rule sets (logical 'or').

AutomationTriggeringRule.description: A rule which is evaluated upon event interception. The rule is configured by comparing a specific value from the event model to an expected value. This comparison is done by using one of the supported operators set.

propertyJPath.description: The JPath of the entity model property that should be checked.

propertyType.description: The data type of the compared operands (string, integer, floating point number or a boolean [true/false]]

operator.description: A valid comparer operator to use. A case-insensitive comparison will be applied for String PropertyType.

AutomationUpdateModel.description: The update model of security automation resource.

AutomationValidationStatus.description: The security automation model state property bag.

isValid.description: Indicates whether the model is valid or not.

Common.CloudError.description: Common error response for all Azure Resource Manager APIs to return error details for failed operations. (This also follows the OData error response format.).

Common.ErrorAdditionalInfo.description: The resource management error additional info.

Common.State.description: Aggregative state based on the standard's supported controls states

values.name: Passed
values.description: All supported regulatory compliance controls in the given standard have a passed state

values.name: Failed
values.description: At least one supported regulatory compliance control in the given standard has a state of failed

values.name: Skipped
values.description: All supported regulatory compliance controls in the given standard have a state of skipped

values.name: Unsupported
values.description: No supported regulatory compliance data for the given standard

values.name: On
values.description: Send notification on new alerts to the subscription's admins

values.name: Off
values.description: Don't send notification on new alerts to the subscription's admins

Common.Tags.description: A list of key value pairs that describe the resource.

tags.description: A list of key value pairs that describe the resource.

MinimalRiskLevel.description: Defines the minimal attack path risk level which will be sent as email notifications

values.name: Critical
values.description: Get notifications on new attack paths with Critical risk level

values.name: High
values.description: Get notifications on new attack paths with High or Critical risk level

values.name: Medium
values.description: Get notifications on new attach paths with Medium, High or Critical risk level

values.name: Low
values.description: Get notifications on new attach paths with Low, Medium, High or Critical risk level

MinimalSeverity.description: Defines the minimal alert severity which will be sent as email notifications

values.name: High
values.description: Get notifications on new alerts with High severity

values.name: Medium
values.description: Get notifications on new alerts with Medium or High severity

values.name: Low
values.description: Get notifications on new alerts with Low, Medium or High severity

sourceType.description: The source type that will trigger the notification

minimalSeverity.description: Defines the minimal alert severity which will be sent as email notifications

minimalRiskLevel.description: Defines the minimal attach path risk level which will be sent as email notifications

Operator.description: A valid comparer operator to use. A case-insensitive comparison will be applied for String PropertyType.

values.name: Equals
values.description: Applies for decimal and non-decimal operands

values.name: NotEquals
values.description: Applies  for decimal and non-decimal operands

PropertyType.description: The data type of the compared operands (string, integer, floating point number or a boolean [true/false]]

SecurityContact.description: Contact details and configurations for notifications coming from Microsoft Defender for Cloud.

value.description: The SecurityContact items on this page

nextLink.description: The link to the next page of items

emails.description: List of email addresses which will get notifications from Microsoft Defender for Cloud by the configurations defined in this security contact.

isEnabled.description: Indicates whether the security contact is enabled.

notificationsSources.description: A collection of sources types which evaluate the email notification.

notificationsByRole.description: Defines whether to send email notifications from Microsoft Defender for Cloud to persons with specific RBAC roles on the subscription.

SecurityContactPropertiesNotificationsBy.description: Defines whether to send email notifications from Microsoft Defender for Cloud to persons with specific RBAC roles on the subscription.

state.description: Defines whether to send email notifications from AMicrosoft Defender for Cloud to persons with specific RBAC roles on the subscription.

roles.description: Defines which RBAC roles will get email notifications from Microsoft Defender for Cloud. List of allowed RBAC roles:

SecurityContactRole.description: A possible role to configure sending security notification alerts to

values.name: AccountAdmin
values.description: If enabled, send notification on new alerts to the account admins

values.name: ServiceAdmin
values.description: If enabled, send notification on new alerts to the service admins

values.name: Owner
values.description: If enabled, send notification on new alerts to the subscription owners

values.name: Contributor
values.description: If enabled, send notification on new alerts to the subscription contributors

SourceType.description: The source type that will trigger the notification
